---
title: "ICPSR 2026 — Introduction to Python"
---

A one-week introduction to Python for social scientists, taught at the
[ICPSR Summer Program in Quantitative Methods](https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/web/pages/sumprog/index.html).
The course assumes no prior coding background and focuses on the practical
skills social scientists need: data manipulation, web scraping, API usage,
and basic natural language processing.

## Slides

- [Day 1 — Getting started with Python](day-1.html)
- [Day 2 — Flow control and data types](day-2.html)
- [Day 3 — Functions and object-oriented programming](day-3.html)
- [Day 4 — NumPy, pandas, and APIs](day-4.html)
- [Day 5 — Web scraping and text-as-data](day-5.html)

## Syllabus

Syllabus PDF: *coming soon* — there are two parallel sections; the
canonical version will be linked here once finalized.

## Source

The source `.qmd` files live in a separate repository; rendered HTML is
hosted here. If you spot an error or have a suggestion, email me.
